authoritative_sources.title: CMS Long-Term Services and Supports and HCBS
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS defines service categories and access assumptions for long-term and community-based supports that inform when elder-care access requests can proceed.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service access framing, program model assumptions, and intake readiness mapping

authoritative_sources.title: CMS HCBS 1915(c) waiver guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Waiver documents define authorization, service limits, and referral pathways that are first-order for community access readiness.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS State Operations Manual (long-term care sections)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The manual establishes operational recordkeeping and oversight expectations for care-support workflows where evidence control is critical.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Eligibility, managed care and provider networks
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Managed-care operations define payer and provider routing implications relevant to who can authorize which service channel.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: System-of-record dependency mapping and ownership routing

authoritative_sources.title: SSA disability process references
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Disability terminology and evidence expectations appear in community referrals and identity/context interpretation across elder-disability operations.

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA minimum necessary standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Record-sharing and consent limits are legally governed by HIPAA and define hard refusal boundaries for third-party actions.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Health Information Technology and care coordination disclosures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Quality and complaint-response references support failure-to-reroute and escalation conditions where community access handling cannot proceed.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and qualification
workflow_stages.description: Collect and normalize identity, residency, referral, and consent context before deciding in-scope status.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Eligibility and authorization readiness
workflow_stages.description: Check referral completeness, benefit pathway, prior authorization, reservation, and access gating records before downstream action.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Privacy and consent control
workflow_stages.description: Apply minimum-necessary sharing and explicit authority checks before processing third-party or consent-dependent actions.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Routing and escalation
workflow_stages.description: Hand off to adjacent specialist, or escalate when evidence, state, or conflict context is missing.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Referral evidence packet (referrer details, service need, and case context)
ILP, PASRR, or care pathway references
systems_of_record: Referral intake and community-access intake systems
State or facility policy rule repositories

allowed: Validate minimum prerequisites and produce explicit intake/readiness outcomes.
Produce routing, escalation, and artifact gap outputs for downstream lanes.
Escalate when tenant policy context is missing or uncertain.
escalate: Missing program/state context that changes eligibility path
Conflicting source guidance that changes routing or authorization decisions
Identity or consent mismatch on third-party access requests
refuse: Requests for clinical authorization, policy exceptions, or financial approvals
Bypass attempts that start booking without complete prerequisites
Legal interpretation or formal entitlement adjudication without licensed authority

domain_failure_modes: Starting dispatch, transportation, or service work without residency and identity confirmation.
Applying a single HCBS/waiver interpretation across states without tenant context retrieval.
Accepting stale or missing authorization/reservation records from the wrong source of truth.
Misdirecting clinical or legal requests into this intake lane and overpromising outcomes.
Allowing third-party actions without explicit consent or role-bound access controls.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-level PASRR and waiver intake sequencing
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different jurisdictions may require distinct referral evidence order and reviewer signoffs.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-policy retrieval for the active state before finalizing readiness.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Meaning of reservation artifacts in local community networks
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: ‘Reservation’ may represent waitlist, pre-booking hold, or direct entitlement hold depending on provider model.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Map tenant terminology to a canonical schema in preflight retrieval and explicitly note assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Third-party access boundaries for guardians vs care coordinators
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Consent authority may differ by tenancy and policy regime.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Block processing with explicit consent mismatch escalation until tenant role matrix is available.

notes: Research is complete enough for validated packaging because all unresolved ambiguity items are explicitly tracked as non-blocking escalations.
Public references are used only as baseline and always constrained by tenant state/program policy.
Industry terms and artifacts drive routing decisions instead of generic intake logic.
